basics.name: Yun Hong
basics.summary: B.S. candidate in Computer Science at Fudan University and exchange student at UT Austin. Research interests include machine learning, robotics, and autonomous driving.

work.name: Fudan University, Natural Language Processing Group
work.summary: Research on ChatGPT-o1 model reproduction and reflection ability improvement under Prof. Xuanjing Huang.
highlights: Synthesized and trained self-critic data based on Llama3.1-8b-instruct
Generated reflection answers from wrong to right using diverse strategies
Constructed complex reflection paths with tree-search reasoning
Evaluated model performance on GSM8K benchmarks

work.name: MagicLab, Fudan University
work.summary: Learning risk maps for autonomous driving in partially observable environments under Wenchao Ding.
highlights: Engineered dynamic risk field representations with spatiotemporal modeling
Developed traffic scene generation with diffusion models
Designed a lightweight network for efficient risk prediction
Research submitted to IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RAL)

education.institution: The University of Texas at Austin

publications.name: Learning Risk Map for Autonomous Driving in Partially Observable Environments
publications.summary: Research on dynamic risk map learning for autonomous driving under partial observability.

portfolio.name: LCDP-Sim
portfolio.description: Language-conditioned diffusion policy system for robotic control.

portfolio.name: MapNavigation
portfolio.description: Navigation system combining OpenStreetMap and Gaode Maps API.

portfolio.name: NeuralStyle
portfolio.description: Modular neural style transfer toolkit with web interface.
